title: SignStream-Eval emission log (schema v1.0)
description: Structural field rules of the streaming-evaluation protocol's emission-log contract. A run consists of two files: run_meta.json (exactly one JSON object; validate it against #/$defs/run_meta) and emissions.jsonl (one JSON object per line; validate each line against #/$defs/event). The top-level object here is the combined in-memory form. Unknown extra fields are allowed everywhere (forward compatibility: a schema-version patch adds optional fields; a minor bump adds new line types). Ordering rules that span multiple lines (t_frame non-decreasing per utterance, exactly one final emission per utterance and last, utt_start before emissions, reference-split coverage) cannot be expressed in JSON Schema and are enforced by the reference validator (signstream.schema).

run_meta.description: The single JSON object in run_meta.json: full provenance for one run.

schema_version.description: Contract version the log was written against (semver, major.minor or major.minor.patch). This schema document describes version 1.0.

created_utc.description: Creation time in ISO-8601 UTC with a 'Z' suffix, e.g. 2026-08-14T09:31:07Z.

git_sha.description: Commit of the producing code, 7-40 lowercase hex digits; runs from a dirty working tree carry a '-dirty' suffix.

config_hash.description: First 8 hex digits of the composed run config's hash.

fps.description: Frame rate of the corpus's logical frame clock.

unit.description: The protocol's per-dataset token decision, echoed into every log.

mask.description: The matched-variant switch. chunk_frames/lookahead_frames are null for the bidirectional offline topline (k = infinity).

hardware.description: Free-form hardware block (gpu, driver, torch, deterministic, ...). Keys are not fixed by the contract: CPU-only and third-party runs record different hardware.

wallclock_protocol.description: How computational latency was measured (protocol reference values: batch_size 1, warmup_utts 10, timer perf_counter).

event.description: One emissions.jsonl line: either of the two v1.0 line types.

utt_start.description: Announces one utterance before any of its emissions and fixes its frame count.

emission.description: One full hypothesis snapshot (never a diff). An emission at t_frame means 'hypothesis available after observing frame t_frame'.

hyp.description: Full unit sequence (units per dataset.unit); may be empty for empty-output utterances.
